## 📦 Skill bundles (dependent suites — promote as a unit)

| Bundle | Repo | Author | Kind | Steps | Why included (reasoning) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| spec-driven-dev-pipeline | `JohannesRabauer/junie-midi-javafx-app` | Johannes Rabauer | workflow | 01-spec → 02-criteria → 03-rules → 04-review → 05-tasks → 06-execute | Six numbered SKILL.md files form one end-to-end spec-driven development pipeline (proposal → spec → criteria → rules → review → tasks → execute). Each step reads files produced by its predecessor and makes no sense in isolation — a textbook Rule 4 bundle. Total content is ~1040 lines of substantive, language-agnostic guidance: EARS-based acceptance criteria with B-N → AC-N → RULE-N traceability, an Ecosystem Survey gate for technology decisions, a severity-tagged cross-document review step that produces a PASS/FAIL verdict gating the task step, YAML task output with dependency graph and checkpoints, and a checkpoint-halting execution loop. No JVM-specific content, but the Rule 5 escape applies — this is a coherent, high-quality workflow bundle, not a thin single step. The host repo (junie-midi-javafx-app, 0 stars) is an app project, but Rule 1 bars that as a reject signal; the skills make zero reference to JavaFX or MIDI and are fully reusable. |
| spec-driven-development | `antonarhipov/agentskills` | Anton Arhipov | workflow | 01-spec → 02-criteria → 03-rules → 04-review → 05-tasks → 06-execute | A tight 6-step spec-driven development pipeline (spec → criteria → rules → review → tasks → execute) authored by Anton Arhipov in his dedicated skills collection (13 stars). Every step explicitly declares its pipeline position and reads the previous step's output (spec/proposal.md → spec/spec.md → spec/criteria.md → spec/rules.md → spec/review.md → spec/tasks.yaml), forming a single indivisible suite. Individual skill files are 79–228 lines with rich, opinionated guidance — EARS acceptance-criteria templates with traceability IDs, RFC-2119 constraint language, a severity-graded review protocol, YAML task-list schema, and a full execution-loop with Checkpoint/Blocker report formats. Per rule 5, a coherent, high-quality workflow bundle is directory-worthy under the workflow category; this one easily meets that bar and is the canonical copy (highest stars, most steps, deepest content) per rule 2. _(also copied in: kotlinconf-sdd, midifx, sdd-demo)_ |
| spec-driven-development-workshop | `antonarhipov/sdd-workshop` | Anton Arhipov | workflow | 01-clarify → 02-analyze → 03-constrain → 04-validate → 05-generate-plan | A 5-step workshop/teaching variant of the same SDD pipeline (clarify → analyze → constrain → validate → generate-plan), thinner per member (36–83 lines each vs 79–228 lines in the canonical) and hosted in a workshop repo (2 stars vs 13). The pipeline is cohesive — each step reads the previous step's artifact — but this is clearly a teaching/conference variant of the canonical found in agentskills. Per the Devnexus 2026 rule, the canonical multi-step pipeline (agentskills, 6 steps, richer content) is promoted as found; this workshop variant is needs_review at best and should not become a second found listing for the same suite. _(also copied in: sdd-workshop)_ |

## ♻️ Real but non-JVM skills (reviewable) (3389)

Well-formed, reusable skills that are **not JVM-specific** — generic agent/dev workflow, external-service integrations, or non-JVM tech. Surfaced for review (e.g. a separate directory).

## 🔍 Needs human classification (the promotion queue) (275)

Real SKILL.md files the auto-classifier could not judge JVM-vs-offtopic — read and promote the JVM ones.

| Skill | Repo | Author | Reason | Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AGENTS.md | `AdamBien/confex-ai` | Adam Bien | review | Opus recheck rejected promotion: AGENTS.md is a project-orientation doc for AI agents in this specific repo; BCE content is a 3-bullet summary pointing to external resources (bce.design, airails SKILL.md) where the real skill lives, and the rest is project-specific module layout and build commands.. (judge: Quarkus MicroProfile BCE template (83 lines) mixing project-specific scaffolding (module layout, build commands) with reusable BCE architectural patterns. Borderline hybrid per rule learnings: contains both architecture guidance AND project-specific paths. Needs review to confirm if reusable BCE content outweighs project-coupling.) |
| AGENTS.md | `AdamBien/quarkus-microprofile` | Adam Bien | review | Opus recheck rejected promotion: Project-specific AGENTS.md (build commands, module layout for this repo only); canonical reusable MicroProfile/BCE skill content lives in AdamBien/airails bce/microprofile-server/SKILL.md which is far richer and already the authoritative source.. (judge: Quarkus MicroProfile BCE template (61 lines) with similar structure to confex-ai. Primarily project-specific scaffolding with BCE architecture touches. Similar hybrid boundary. Adam Bien's airails repo contains canonical microprofile-server/SKILL.md; this may be a thinner variant. Needs review to assess whether it duplicates the canonical or adds distinct reusable content.) |
| java-modernizer | `AdamBien/zsmith` | Adam Bien | review | Opus recheck rejected promotion: Lives in src/test/skills (a test fixture for the zsmith agent framework, not a standalone skill), only 16 lines with no concrete examples or code, and provides no guidance beyond prompting an LLM to identify Java 21+ patterns it already knows — too thin and context-specific to be a promotable reusable skill.. (judge: JVM-specific skill teaching modernization of Java patterns (records, pattern matching, sealed types, switch expressions) — real guidance but very limited depth (16 lines) and breadth. Borderline whether it qualifies as a promotable skill vs. a template stub.) |
| docs-style-check | `AgentEnder/cli-forge` | Craigory Coppola | review | Opus recheck rejected promotion: Hardcoded to CLI Forge's own doc structure (docs-site/docs/, Vale config, mise exec) and not JVM-specific at all -- project-bound tooling, not a reusable JVM skill.. (judge: Documentation style checking skill with structured rules. Real content but framework-agnostic tool guidance (Markdown, prose style), not JVM-specific. Borderline reusable.) |
| brief | `AgentEnder/design-drafts` | Craigory Coppola | review | Opus recheck rejected promotion: Real, well-built Socratic-interview skill for writing design briefs, but it is a general web/design-drafting methodology with zero JVM or backend-development relevance, failing the JVM-specific bar.. (judge: Design brief creation skill with structured process. Real skill content (depth 2) but design-focused, not JVM development. Borderline reusable methodology.) |
| explore | `AgentEnder/design-drafts` | Craigory Coppola | review | Opus recheck rejected promotion: Genuine, well-crafted skill but purely UI/frontend design-exploration workflow (themes/layouts/visual references) with no JVM/backend relevance.. (judge: Design exploration workflow with variant testing. Real skill (depth 2) with good process guidance, but UI/design-focused, not applicable to JVM backend work.) |
| variants | `AgentEnder/design-drafts` | Craigory Coppola | review | Opus recheck rejected promotion: Well-written but purely front-end HTML/CSS design-draft tooling (plugin skill for generating UI variant pages) with zero JVM/Java/Kotlin relevance, so it fails the JVM-specific reusability bar despite genuine depth.. | use-analysis | `franz1981/ClaudeFranzBFF` | Francesco Nigro | review | Opus recheck rejected promotion: Reusability limited to perf-lab log format; JVM-specificity is incidental (analyzes JVM processes via vmstat/mpstat/pidstat) rather than JVM-native (GC, JIT, heap, threads) — the core USE method is generic Linux system analysis that happens to target JVM processes.. (judge: Real performance analysis methodology (USE method) applied to JVM benchmarking context. Content shows understanding of system metrics (CPU utilization, saturation, errors), concrete column positions in performance tools, and perf-lab result interpretation. JVM fit is medium: perf-lab is JVM-specific, but vmstat/pidstat are generic system tools. Depth is solid (252 lines with structured steps) but scope is narrow: benchmark result analysis rather than general JVM performance diagnosis. Candidate for promotion if the scope can extend beyond perf-lab, or if perf-lab is the canonical benchmark framework for JVM performance work in the community. Needs review to clarify whether benchmark-specific guidance meets 'reusable skill' bar.) |

| AGENTS.md | `uta8a/curatedz` | Masanori Tani | review | Opus recheck rejected promotion: Pure project-doc: Rust Lambda + TypeScript CDK stack (not JVM), all guidance is repo-specific (this project's S3 layout, GitHub Discussion output format, HMAC feedback form, specific IAM policy), no transferable JVM skill content.. (judge: 95-line hybrid AGENTS.md mixing project-specific scaffolding (infrastructure, S3 layout, GitHub output format) with some architectural patterns (RSS pipeline, Step Functions orchestration, Rust Lambda + TypeScript CDK split). Per rule 70 (DPL 2026), hybrid project-docs with embedded skill content remain project-doc unless the transferable guidance is self-contained and primary. Here, guidance is heavily tied to this repo's AWS infrastructure decisions and S3 bucket structure. Classified as needs_review rather than reject because there are genuine patterns (pipeline orchestration, cost-focused choices) that *could* be reusable, but they are not sufficiently extracted or general.) |
